# Executable Conformance Contracts for Tokio-Replacement Tracks

**Bead**: `asupersync-2oh2u.10.2` ([T8.2])
**Generated by**: FuchsiaGorge (codex-cli / gpt-5)
**Date**: 2026-03-03
**Dependencies**: `asupersync-2oh2u.10.1`, `docs/tokio_functional_parity_contract.md`, `docs/tokio_nonfunctional_closure_criteria.md`, `docs/tokio_evidence_checklist.md`, `docs/tokio_deterministic_lab_model_expansion.md`
**Purpose**: Define executable, non-ambiguous conformance contracts with pass/fail semantics, artifact requirements, and CI gate bindings for every high-priority replacement track.

---

## 1. Contract Schema (Normative)

Every executable conformance contract row MUST define all fields below.

| Field | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Contract ID | Stable ID (`EC-*`) used by CI and replay triage. |
| Capability Track | Track mapping (`T2`, `T3`, `T4`, `T5`, `T6`, `T7`, cross-track). |
| Source Contract Input | Link to parity/DoD/model source docs. |
| Runner Command | Deterministic command (cargo/test script) prefixed with `rch exec --` for heavy runs. |
| Pass Criteria | Objective assertions; no ambiguous wording. |
| Failure Class | Structured failure taxonomy key. |
| Required Artifacts | Required outputs (for example `event_log.txt`, `failed_assertions.json`, `repro_manifest.json`, suite `summary.json`). |
| Gate Binding | Which CI gate tier enforces the row. |

Required pass/fail status values are:

- `PASS`: all required assertions and artifacts present.
- `FAIL`: any assertion failure, missing artifact, or schema violation.
- `BLOCKED`: external infrastructure failure (for example remote worker unavailable) with explicit incident reference.

---

## 2. Failure Taxonomy (Normative)

| Failure Class | Meaning | Default Severity |
|---|---|---|
| `semantic_drift` | Behavior differs from functional parity contract. | High |
| `timing_drift` | Deterministic schedule/replay timing expectations diverge. | Medium |
| `cancel_protocol_violation` | Request/drain/finalize semantics broken. | Critical |
| `loser_drain_violation` | Race losers not fully drained. | Critical |
| `obligation_leak` | Permit/ack/lease lifecycle not resolved. | Critical |
| `artifact_schema_violation` | Required artifact missing or schema invalid. | High |
| `authority_flow_violation` | Capability/authority boundary violated. | Critical |
| `interop_boundary_violation` | Adapter behavior violates boundary contract. | High |

---

## 3. Executable Contract Rows

### 3.1 Track T2 (Async IO + tokio-util parity)

| Contract ID | Capability Track | Source Contract Input | Runner Command | Pass Criteria | Failure Class | Required Artifacts | Gate Binding |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `EC-T2-01` | `T2` | `tokio_io_parity_audit` + functional parity D03 | `rch exec -- cargo test --test tokio_io_parity_audit -- --nocapture` | all trait-surface parity contract tests pass | `semantic_drift` | `summary.json`, `event_log.txt` | `Gate-A` |
| `EC-T2-02` | `T2` | T8.1 model domain `DM01` + `LM-S1` | `rch exec -- cargo test --test cancellation_conformance -- --nocapture` | cancel checkpoints preserve no-loss semantics | `cancel_protocol_violation` | `event_log.txt`, `failed_assertions.json`, `repro_manifest.json` | `Gate-B` |

|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|

| `EC-T3-02` | `T3` | T8.1 model domains `DM02`/`DM03`/`DM04` + `LM-S2`/`LM-S3` | `rch exec -- cargo test --test cancellation_conformance -- --nocapture` | lifecycle + shutdown deterministic checks pass | `cancel_protocol_violation` | `event_log.txt`, `failed_assertions.json`, `repro_manifest.json` | `Gate-B` |

### 3.7 Cross-Track Determinism and Replay

| Contract ID | Capability Track | Source Contract Input | Runner Command | Pass Criteria | Failure Class | Required Artifacts | Gate Binding |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `EC-X-01` | `Cross` | T8.1 SC-01..SC-05 + replay policy | `rch exec -- cargo test --test replay_debugging -- --nocapture` | deterministic replay contract checks pass end-to-end | `timing_drift` | `event_log.txt`, `failed_assertions.json`, `repro_manifest.json` | `Gate-C` |
| `EC-X-02` | `Cross` | evidence checklist + non-functional closure criteria | `rch exec -- cargo test --test tokio_nonfunctional_closure_criteria -- --nocapture` | non-functional thresholds and evidence fields remain contract-complete | `artifact_schema_violation` | `summary.json` | `Gate-A` |

---

## 4. CI Gate Semantics

| Gate | Scope | Policy |
|---|---|---|
| `Gate-A` | contract-doc/schema integrity + baseline parity tests | hard fail on any `FAIL` or missing required artifacts |
| `Gate-B` | cancellation/obligation protocol correctness under deterministic scenarios | hard fail on protocol violations; no waive without explicit incident ticket |
| `Gate-C` | replay/determinism/interop boundary hardening | hard fail on `semantic_drift`, `timing_drift`, `authority_flow_violation` |

`BLOCKED` status is only valid with a linked infra incident and automatic re-run scheduling; it cannot be treated as equivalent to `PASS`.

---

## 5. Downstream Binding and Unblock Map

T8.2 output is intended to directly drive downstream tracks:

| Downstream Bead | How this contract spec is consumed |
|---|---|
| `asupersync-2oh2u.10.3` | differential behavior suites consume `EC-*` row IDs as canonical assertions |
| `asupersync-2oh2u.10.4` | fuzz/race campaigns map failure classes and artifact schema from this document |
| `asupersync-2oh2u.10.5` | CI quality gates implement `Gate-A/B/C` semantics and block/waive rules |
| `asupersync-2oh2u.10.6` | replay artifact schema policy binds to required artifact fields and taxonomy |

---

## 6. Exit Criteria for T8.2

T8.2 is complete when:

1. Contract schema is explicit, normative, and machine-enforceable.
2. Contract rows cover T2/T3/T4/T5/T6/T7 plus cross-track determinism.
3. Every row has objective pass/fail semantics and at least one required artifact.
4. Failure taxonomy is reusable by downstream fuzz/replay/CI tracks.
5. Gate mapping to T8.3/T8.4/T8.5/T8.6 is explicit and non-ambiguous.

This specification intentionally separates contract definition from contract execution harness implementation, so downstream beads can consume stable IDs and semantics without reinterpretation.
